rationale: All three homes display multi-faceted hip roofs with ridge lines set approximately 30–40% inward from the eave perimeter, consistent with a 5:12 pitch. Ground shadows cast from the eave edges extend roughly 8–12 feet (estimated via the 50 ft scale bar), which aligns with a moderate pitch at Springfield, MO's latitude (~37°N) in summer sun. Differential shading between north- and south-facing roof planes confirms meaningful slope but not a steep one. The regional context (mid-southern US climate, suburban ranch-style construction) further supports a 5:12 estimate, with 6:12 being a plausible alternate if the shadow projection is slightly underestimated.
